name: Indian Academic Journals — UGC-CARE, Scopus, SCI Indexed
description: Validates citations and references against Indian academic publishing standards. Covers: UGC-CARE listed journals (Group I & II), Scopus-indexed Indian journals, SCI/SCIE indexed, Web of Science, and Indian citation formats. Ensures references are traceable and from legitimate sources — critical for UGC-compliant PhD submission.
ethical_boundary: Do not fabricate citations from non-existent journals. Do not suggest citations from predatory journals. Only recommend real, verified journals from UGC-CARE, Scopus, or Web of Science lists.

rules.description: Cross-reference cited journals against UGC-CARE Group I and Group II lists. Journals not in UGC-CARE, Scopus, or Web of Science are flagged for verification. Critical for UGC compliance.
Flag journals known to be predatory: Omics, SCIgen, WASET, and other fake/predatory publishers. Verify ISSN, impact factor claims, and editorial board legitimacy.
Indian theses commonly use APA 7th or numbered citation format. Verify consistency: all in-text citations match reference list, all DOIs accessible, URLs resolve.
Cross-reference cited sources against Scopus index using ISSN/DOI. Flag sources claiming Scopus indexing without evidence.
Verify conference proceedings citations: IEEE Xplore, Springer LNCS, ACM Digital Library. Indian conferences (INDIACom, ICACCI, etc.) must have verifiable proceedings.
If citing non-English Indian language sources (Hindi, Tamil, Bengali, etc.), verify transliteration consistency and provide English translation in brackets.
Indian PhD submissions must reference Shodhganga (INFLIBNET) for previous theses. Check if related Indian theses are cited from Shodhganga repository.
For open access journal citations, cross-reference against Directory of Open Access Journals (DOAJ). Flag journals claiming OA status without DOAJ listing.
